title: Conjecture 1.1 (Flandrin et al.)
statement_text: If $G$ is a connected graph of order at least three different from the cycle $C_5$, then $\chi'_{\Sigma}(G) \leq \Delta(G) + 2$.
context_text: This conjecture on the neighbour sum distinguishing index was proposed by Flandrin et al. in [14] and verified there for paths, cycles, complete graphs, complete bipartite graphs, and trees. It is the proper-edge-colouring analogue of the 1–2–3 Conjecture and is the central object of study in the paper.
paper_contribution: Proves the stronger bound $\chi'_{\Sigma}(G) \leq \Delta(G)+1$ for every planar graph $G$ with $\Delta(G) \geq 28$ and no isolated edges, which in particular confirms Conjecture 1.1 for this infinite family.
notes: PDF source — the paper uses both $\chi'_P(G)$ and $\chi'_{\Sigma}(G)$ for the same invariant; notation may be garbled by PDF extraction.
paper_title: On the neighbour sum distinguishing index of planar graphs

title: Conjecture 1.3 (Zhang, Liu and Wang)
statement_text: If $G$ is a connected graph of order at least three different from the cycle $C_5$, then $\chi'_a(G) \leq \Delta(G) + 2$.
context_text: The neighbour set distinguishing index (adjacent strong chromatic index) conjecture was introduced by Zhang, Liu and Wang in 2002 and is described in the paper as a weaker protoplast of Conjecture 1.1. The main result is presented as a significant strengthening of Bonamy–Bousquet–Hocquard's result $\chi'_a(G) \leq \Delta(G)+1$ for planar graphs.
paper_contribution: Implies Conjecture 1.3 for planar graphs with $\Delta(G) \geq 28$ as a corollary, since $\chi'_a(G) \leq \chi'_{\Sigma}(G)$ and the paper establishes $\chi'_{\Sigma}(G) \leq \Delta(G)+1$ for this class.
notes: PDF source — Conjecture 1.3 is a secondary consequence; the paper's primary focus is Conjecture 1.1.
paper_title: On the neighbour sum distinguishing index of planar graphs
